summary: Department of Labor video set to music, built from desaturated sepia and black and white historical paintings (Founding-era figures, colonial soldiers, a gold George Washington equestrian statue, sailing ships, a young serviceman looking upward) with word-by-word animated captions. A narration says ancestors 'climbed mountains, crossed deserts, never gave up.'

script.scene: Sepia and black and white painting of Founding-era figures (a constitutional convention scene); animated white caption fades in: 'OUR ANCESTORS CLIMBED'; music and narration begin.
Desaturated montage layering colonial figures with a gold statue and a soldier on horseback; caption 'CROSSED DESERTS,' narration: 'crossed deserts...'
Ranks of colonial soldiers with a Revolutionary flag and the gold Washington equestrian statue; caption 'WIDE OPEN PLAINS.'
Close-up of a young uniformed serviceman looking upward over a smoky battlefield, gold statue in the foreground; caption 'WHO NEVER GAVE UP,' narration: 'champions who never gave up...'
Gold statue of Washington on horseback in silhouette against a misty harbor with a tall sailing ship; caption 'EVER GAVE IN,' narration: 'who never ever gave in.'

notable_text: OUR ANCESTORS CLIMBED; CROSSED DESERTS; WIDE OPEN PLAINS; WHO NEVER GAVE UP; EVER GAVE IN; (tweet) One American Heritage.
